scope.name: MutationSurfaceRegistry
scope.purpose: Define the canonical static registry of governed mutation surfaces consumed by the SCO_VALID transition. The registry is the authoritative source for mutation class, target surface, and risk class classification of governed file paths.

registry_principle.conflict_resolution: Registry wins on conflict. Dynamic scan evidence does not override registry classification.
registry_principle.unknown_consumer_rule: A file path not present in the registry must result in GOVERNANCE_UNKNOWN -> SCO_INVALID.
registry_principle.undeclared_consumer_rule: A consumer discovered by dynamic scan but not present in the registry must result in SCO_INVALID.

entry_model.path_pattern_semantics: Prefix match. A pattern ending with '/' matches any path beginning with that prefix. An exact pattern matches only the exact path.
entry_model.mixed_purpose_rule: When a file matches multiple registry entries, the entry with the highest risk_class wins. When consumers disagree, risk wins.

canonical_entries.note: Includes documentation files under governance/. Files in governance/ are classified as governance_mutation regardless of file type.

fail_closed_rule: Any governed file path not matched by a canonical entry must be classified GOVERNANCE_UNKNOWN and the SCO_VALID transition must produce SCO_INVALID.

drift_detection.principle: Dynamic scan is evidence-only. It does not grant legitimacy.
